evals.expected_output: Accessibility audit that uses lighthouse_audit + snapshot for semantics, reports with remediation.
assertions: Uses accessibility tree (snapshot) as primary evidence.
Runs lighthouse_audit in navigation mode when safe.
Reports failures with observed/expected/remediation; uses uid or selector.

evals.prompt: The signup form labels are missing for screen readers, please audit focus and ARIA.
evals.expected_output: Implicit browser a11y request activates the specialized skill.
assertions: Agent routes to a11y-debugging based on ARIA/focus/keyboard signals.
Prefers accessibility tree over generic debugging.
Does not hand off to main chrome-devtools.

evals.prompt: Debug console errors on the checkout page.
evals.expected_output: Negative control routes generic console/network debugging away from a11y skill.
assertions: Agent does not claim a11y skill for console error work.
Does not run lighthouse accessibility unless a11y concern stated.

evals.prompt: /chrome-devtools-a11y-debugging test tab order on the modal dialog
evals.expected_output: Uses repeated press_key + fresh snapshots to verify focus order.
assertions: Agent verifies keyboard by stepping with press_key and snapshots.
Checks focus trap and return after close for modals.
Does not rely on screenshot alone for focus.

evals.prompt: /chrome-devtools-a11y-debugging run full lighthouse and include the entire report
evals.expected_output: Refuses to load entire Lighthouse JSON; extracts failed audits only.
assertions: Agent cites rule: do not load entire Lighthouse reports into context.
Uses targeted extraction (e.g. node -e or parser) for failed audits.
Still provides remediation focused on a11y failures.

evals.prompt: /chrome-devtools-a11y-debugging take screenshots of all contrast issues
evals.expected_output: Screenshots only as supporting; snapshot/tree is primary.
assertions: Agent prefers accessibility tree and snippets for labels/contrast.
Uses screenshots only to support, never as primary evidence.
Cites: Use screenshots only as supporting visual evidence.
